steps.title: Recognize the y-intercept
steps.content: The point \((0, 8)\) lies on the line. Since \(x = 0\) at this point, \(y = 8\) is the \(y\)-intercept.

steps.title: Write the equation
steps.content: With slope \(m = 6\) and \(y\)-intercept \(b = 8\), slope-intercept form gives: $$y = 6x + 8$$

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '\(y = 6x + 8\)': Slope 6 and \(y\)-intercept 8 — both match. ✓
  • Choice B — '\(y = 6x + 48\)': Slope 6 is correct, but \(y\)-intercept 48 does not match 8. ✗
  • Choice C — '\(y = 8x + 6\)': Slope 8 is wrong (should be 6) and \(y\)-intercept 6 is wrong (should be 8). ✗
  • Choice D — '\(y = 8x + 48\)': Both slope and \(y\)-intercept are incorrect. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
